name: Tennessee Information Protection Act
summary: The Tennessee Information Protection Act establishes consumer data privacy rights for Tennessee residents. It applies to entities conducting business in Tennessee or targeting its residents that exceed $25 million in revenue and control or process personal information of at least 25,000 consumers, or derive more than 50% of revenue from selling personal information of at least 175,000 consumers.
keyRequirements: Right to confirm whether a controller is processing personal information and to access that data
Right to correct inaccuracies in personal information
Right to delete personal information provided by or obtained about the consumer
Right to obtain a copy of personal information in a portable format
Right to opt out of sale of personal information, targeted advertising, and profiling
Affirmative defense available for controllers maintaining a privacy program conforming to NIST frameworks
Consent required before processing sensitive data such as biometric, geolocation, and health data
penalties: Enforced exclusively by the Tennessee Attorney General. Civil penalties up to $7,500 per violation. 60-day cure period for violations; no private right of action.
